title: Aster DM Healthcare Hospitals earn top spots in Newsweek’s World’s Best Hospitals 2026 rankings
summary: A defining feature of the ranking methodology is its reliance on globally recognized tools such as Patient-Reported Outcome Measures (PROMs) and Patient-Reported Experience Measures (PREMs).
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
UAE—Aster DM Healthcare has strengthened its international reputation after four of its hospitals secured positions in the 2026 edition of the World’s Best Hospitals ranking by Newsweek.

The recognition reflects consistent performance in patient care, clinical outcomes, and overall healthcare delivery across its network.

Notably, the group’s continued presence on the list highlights a sustained commitment to quality rather than a one-time achievement.

In the United Arab Emirates, Aster Hospital Mankhool ranked fourth, followed by Aster Hospital Al Qusais at tenth and Medcare Hospital – Al Safa at twenty-fifth.

Meanwhile, Aster Sanad Hospital in Saudi Arabia secured the fortieth position.

These facilities have also appeared in previous editions of the rankings, as well as in Newsweek’s World’s Best Smart Hospitals 2026 list, reinforcing their consistent performance and focus on innovation.

Patient-centered metrics driving performance

A defining feature of the ranking methodology is its reliance on globally recognized tools such as Patient-Reported Outcome Measures (PROMs) and Patient-Reported Experience Measures (PREMs).

Aster DM Healthcare has embedded these frameworks into its operational model, using them not merely as satisfaction indicators but as critical drivers of quality improvement.

By systematically capturing patient feedback, the group aligns its services with real-world outcomes, thereby strengthening clinical effectiveness, safety standards, and overall patient experience.

The ranking itself is developed in collaboration with Statista and incorporates insights from medical professionals, large-scale patient surveys, and key performance indicators.

These metrics evaluate hospitals across several dimensions, including clinical quality, patient safety, and operational efficiency, ensuring a comprehensive global benchmarking process.

Leadership perspective and regional growth

Commenting on the achievement, Alisha Moopen, Managing Director and Group CEO of Aster DM Healthcare, emphasized that the recognition reflects the organization’s scale and consistency.

She noted that eight hospitals across the GCC and India received acknowledgment this year, underscoring the group’s ability to deliver high standards across diverse markets.

She further highlighted the company’s strategy of integrating clinical excellence with a patient-centered approach, supported by structured use of outcome and experience data.

Recent developments strengthening market position

In recent days, Aster DM Healthcare has also advanced its regional strategy by expanding service offerings and launching digital health initiatives to improve accessibility and care coordination.

The company continues to invest in technology-driven healthcare delivery, including AI-supported diagnostics and integrated care platforms, to enhance efficiency and patient outcomes across its facilities.

Additionally, the group has reinforced its focus on specialized care services and network expansion within the GCC, aligning with growing demand for high-quality, affordable healthcare solutions.

These developments complement its strong performance in global rankings and reflect ongoing efforts to modernize healthcare delivery while maintaining patient-centric values.
